perturbation.original_text: This Agreement provides for Continued Service and for Netzee to provide basic maintenance in support following expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. The Continued Service provided by Netzee will be the operation, maintenance, and support of the computer systems, software and interfaces included in the System.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement provides for Enhanced Service and for Netzee to provide premium maintenance in support following expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. The Continued Service provided by Netzee will be the operation, maintenance, and support of the computer systems, software and interfaces included in the System.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Continued Service' to 'Enhanced Service' and 'basic maintenance' to 'premium maintenance', it introduces ambiguity. While the agreement later refers to 'Continued Service', it's now unclear if this refers to the newly introduced 'Enhanced Service' or if 'Continued Service' and 'Enhanced Service' are indeed different things. This is reinforced due to the agreement not using 'Enhanced Service' once again.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement provides for Enhanced Service and for Netzee to provide premium maintenance in support following expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. The Continued Service provided by Netzee will be the operation, maintenance, and support of the computer systems, software and interfaces included in the System. Bankers Bank agrees to pay fees equal to $40 per month for all Participating Banks (original or added) for which maintenance is provided. The maintenance is for a period of one (1) year commencing upon expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. Following the completion of such maintenance term, Bankers Bank may, at its option, renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee not to exceed 5% at least sixty (60) days in advance of the applicable renewal date. Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Enhanced Service' and 'premium maintenance' while the contradicted text only refers to 'Continued Service'. The explanation highlights that the agreement then only refers to 'Continued Service' creating a contradiction, as it is unclear whether 'Continued Service' is equivalent to 'Enhanced Service' or a separate offering. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict, and it adheres to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, because the agreement uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them.

perturbation.original_text: Maintenance provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, standard maintenance upgrades and modifications offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.changed_text: Servicing provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Maintenance' to 'Servicing', 'standard maintenance upgrades and modifications' to 'routine servicing enhancements and alterations' introduces ambiguity because the contract initially defines the agreement as a 'Maintenance Agreement' and refers to 'maintenance' throughout. The change implies 'Servicing' is a different offering, which makes it unclear if all references to 'maintenance' throughout the document still apply, and creates uncertainty on if they can use a different term for the same obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement provides for Enhanced Service and for Netzee to provide premium maintenance in support following expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. The Continued Service provided by Netzee will be the operation, maintenance, and support of the computer systems, software and interfaces included in the System. Bankers Bank agrees to pay fees equal to $40 per month for all Participating Banks (original or added) for which maintenance is provided. The maintenance is for a period of one (1) year commencing upon expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. Following the completion of such maintenance term, Bankers Bank may, at its option, renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee not to exceed 5% at least sixty (60) days in advance of the applicable renewal date. Servicing provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Maintenance' to 'Servicing' and 'standard maintenance upgrades and modifications' to 'routine servicing enhancements and alterations' introduces ambiguity that directly contradicts the section referring to 'maintenance'. The contradicted text refers to the agreement providing 'premium maintenance' and specifies fees related to 'maintenance'. By using 'Servicing' instead of 'Maintenance' in the altered text, it becomes unclear whether the provisions and fees related to 'maintenance' still apply to 'Servicing', creating a contradiction. The use of multiple terms interchangeably without clear definition creates conflicting obligations. It adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN.
perturbation.changed_text: THIS SERVICING ADDENDUM IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT' to 'SERVICING ADDENDUM' creates contradiction because the agreement is named 'Maintenance Agreement' at the beginning. Using the word 'ADDENDUM' also impacts, because this word specifies that it adds to another document, which in this case is the 'Master Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement provides for Enhanced Service and for Netzee to provide premium maintenance in support following expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. The Continued Service provided by Netzee will be the operation, maintenance, and support of the computer systems, software and interfaces included in the System. Bankers Bank agrees to pay fees equal to $40 per month for all Participating Banks (original or added) for which maintenance is provided. The maintenance is for a period of one (1) year commencing upon expiration of the initial one (1) year term of the License/Services Schedule. Following the completion of such maintenance term, Bankers Bank may, at its option, renew maintenance for subsequent periods of one (1) year each, subject to adjustments proposed by Netzee not to exceed 5% at least sixty (60) days in advance of the applicable renewal date. Servicing provided by Netzee during the term of this Agreement shall include (1) routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, routine servicing enhancements and alterations offered by Netzee to its customers generally, and for such purpose Netzee will use commercially reasonable efforts to make and include (without limitation) as such modifications changes and upgrades necessary to comply with generally applicable industry and regulatory requirements of which it is notified (2) 5x12 call-in support between hours of 6:30 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. Eastern Time Monday through Friday, (3) in the event of material discrepancies between the Services as provided and the specifications for such Services (which shall be furnished in reasonably acceptable form), reasonable effort (in relation to importance, impact, and scheduled development plans) to correct those discrepancies, and (4) availability of basic Services with at least 99% uptime between 4:00 a.m Monday and 7:00 p.m. Friday and between 8:00 a.m. Saturday and 2:00 p.m. Saturday Eastern Time, exclusive of outages caused by off-peak prescheduled maintenance or causes beyond Netzee's reasonable control (including third-party equipment or communications). THIS SERVICING ADDENDUM IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN. SUCH TERMS AND CONDITIONS ARE HEREBY AFFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE IN THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ALL LICENSES GRANTED OR EXERCISABLE THEREUNDER. COMMENCEMENT DATE FOR MAINTENANCE UNDER THIS ADDENDUM: MARCH 1, 2001 1 IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned duly authorized representatives of the parties hereto have made and entered in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to a 'MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT'. The changed text replaces this with 'SERVICING ADDENDUM'. The contradicted text contains the sentence 'THIS SERVICING ADDENDUM IS GOVERNED BY, AND SHALL BE SUBJECT TO, THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE MASTER AGREEMENT BETWEEN NETZEE AND BANKERS BANK, INCLUDING THE LIMITED WARRANTY AND DISCLAIMER AND LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY PROVIDED THEREIN. SUCH TERMS AND CONDITIONS ARE HEREBY AFFIRMED BY BOTH PARTIES AND INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE IN THIS MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT'. This sentence explicitly ties the 'SERVICING ADDENDUM' to a 'MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT', creating a direct contradiction, because they are presented as the same thing, but the changed text renames the original 'MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT' to 'SERVICING ADDENDUM'. Also the word 'ADDENDUM' implies it's an addition to the Master Agreement, while the original text implies the 'MAINTENANCE AGREEMENT' is a standalone document.
